reasoning: Historically, the US stock market exhibits a positive upward drift, with roughly a 62-65% chance of posting a positive return over any given 3-month period. When adjusting for seasonality, the period from late October through late January encompasses November and December—historically two of the strongest months of the year for equities—as well as the often-positive 'January effect.' Consequently, the likelihood of a positive return over this specific ~3-month window is elevated compared to the unconditional base rate. Estimating a slightly higher mean return for this seasonally strong period yields a probability of approximately 68%. While short-term macroeconomic volatility or unforeseen shocks pose downside risks, the base rate heavily favors an increase.
